to.note: Decisions binding on claude-control (infrastructure), psychology-agent (domain knowledge transfer), and future operations-agent

subject: Operations-agent standup — all 6 questions resolved, emerging architecture decisions included

body.preamble: Human arbiter reviewed claude-control's proposal (turn 1) and psychology-agent's response (turn 2). All 6 open questions resolved. Two additional architectural directions emerged from the review process and are included as binding decisions.

resolved_questions.detail: The interagent dashboard (index.html + worker.js) moves to the operations-agent repo. Operations-agent owns layout, visual consistency, and the deploy pipeline. Other agents contribute via PRs or by serving data at /api/* endpoints. Discovery uses .well-known/agent-card.json — the compositor reads agent-cards at startup instead of maintaining a hardcoded AGENTS array.
resolved_questions.rejects: Shared repo (too many owners, coordination overhead at current scale). Psychology-agent repo (historical accident, not design).

resolved_questions.detail: Operations-agent owns both index.html and worker.js as a unit. The CF Worker remains a thin aggregation proxy. Tab-specific rendering stays client-side. Agents serve structured data at /api/status and /api/kb; the compositor consumes it uniformly.
resolved_questions.rejects: Functional split of Worker logic across agents (unnecessary complexity for a thin proxy).

resolved_questions.detail: Each agent serves .well-known/agent-card.json describing its identity, capabilities, endpoints, and tab contributions. The compositor reads agent-cards at startup for dynamic discovery. No KV-backed registry needed — agent-cards are the registry. A new agent joins the mesh by deploying an agent-card; the compositor discovers it on next refresh.
resolved_questions.rejects: Hardcoded AGENTS array (doesn't scale). KV-backed registry (adds runtime dependency and latency for marginal benefit at 5-6 agents).

resolved_questions.detail: Shared vocabulary (terms that appear in agent-cards, the dashboard, and cross-agent data contracts) is governed by operations-agent. Domain-specific vocabulary stays local to each agent. Psychology-agent governs psychology/psychometrics terms internally. Claude-control governs infrastructure terms internally. When a domain term needs to become shared (e.g., appearing in the compositor), the owning agent proposes it to operations-agent for inclusion in the shared vocabulary.
resolved_questions.rejects: Single-agent governance by psychology-agent (doesn't scale to 5+ domains). Human-only governance (too slow for operational vocabulary evolution).

resolved_questions.detail: Claude-control owns chromabook infrastructure and allocates ports. Current meshd ports: :8076 (psychology), :8077 (psq), :8078 (unratified), :8079 (observatory). Claude-control assigns the operations-agent port based on what is available and avoids common default ports (8080, 3000, etc.) if they conflict with existing services.
resolved_questions.rejects: Human-assigned ports (infrastructure owner knows the bind landscape better).

resolved_questions.detail: safety-quotient-lab/operations-agent created as a private repository. Claude-control's 7-item pre-commit gate checklist applies. Standard .gitignore covers: state.db, state.local.db, lessons.md, .claude/settings.local.json, .env, .dev.vars. Human reviews the repo contents before flipping to public. No timeline pressure — go public only when the audit confirms zero leaks.
resolved_questions.rejects: Public from day one (unnecessary risk during initial standup when configuration is volatile).

emerging_architecture_decisions.detail: git-PR transport stays for human review, public transparency, and durable session records. A real-time messaging layer complements it for inter-agent signaling, event notification, and operational telemetry. The messaging layer does NOT replace git-PR — it handles the cases where git-PR is too slow (sub-second agent coordination, transient state changes, reliability of event delivery).
emerging_architecture_decisions.candidates: Redis pub/sub (pending cabinet verification — cabinet currently offline), ZeroMQ (brokerless, peer-to-peer). MQTT also viable given claude-control's existing mqtt-zulip-bridge. Decision deferred until cabinet is online and Redis availability is confirmed.
emerging_architecture_decisions.constraint: Least infrastructure possible. One additional daemon is acceptable; a full message broker cluster is not.

emerging_architecture_decisions.decision: Web Component decomposition for compositor HTML
emerging_architecture_decisions.detail: The monolithic index.html (~3000 lines) decomposes into Web Components. Each tab becomes a custom element. Agent-cards at .well-known/ include tab contribution manifests (tab name, data endpoints, schema.org types). The compositor shell discovers tabs from agent-cards and loads components dynamically. This enables agent-owned tabs with centralized visual governance by operations-agent.
emerging_architecture_decisions.constraint: DIY Web Components — no framework dependency. Vanilla custom elements with optional JSON-LD structured manifests for semantic discovery.

emerging_architecture_decisions.detail: Operations-agent's deploy pipeline runs through Jenkins (Tier 2 CI/CD). Jenkins → wrangler publish for the CF Worker. Existing Tier 2 infrastructure handles this with no new tooling.
emerging_architecture_decisions.constraint: Must integrate with existing Jenkins setup on cabinet. When cabinet comes back online, verify Jenkins can reach the operations-agent repo (private repo access).

action_items.action: Create safety-quotient-lab/operations-agent as a private GitHub repo. Apply .gitignore template. Allocate meshd port. Set up systemd unit.
Respond to the pending D1 aggregation pipeline request (cabinet-infrastructure session, to-claude-control-001.json). That request remains open and is separate from operations-agent standup.
Prepare cognitive architecture template in platform/shared/ for operations-agent adaptation. Hooks, identity pattern, transport protocol, lessons schema.
Extract compositor (interagent/) from psychology-agent repo. Coordinate with operations-agent on handoff once the repo exists.
Receive compositor codebase. Begin Web Component decomposition. Implement .well-known discovery in worker.js.
Ensure .well-known/agent-card.json includes tab contribution manifests (tab name, data endpoints, schema types) for compositor discovery.
When cabinet comes online: verify Redis availability, evaluate Redis pub/sub vs ZeroMQ vs MQTT for real-time messaging layer.

cross_references.psychology_agent_response: from-psychology-agent-002.json — positions accepted with modifications noted above. Protocol compliance notes acknowledged (claude-control should add v19 fields to future messages).
cross_references.cabinet_infrastructure: Separate session. D1 pipeline request remains open. Not blocked by operations-agent decisions.
cross_references.self_readiness_audit: R3 gate remains open. Separate concern. Option A (remediate + R4) preferred but not decided here.

action_gate.gate_condition: claude-control acknowledges decisions and begins operations-agent repo creation

epistemic_flags: Cabinet offline — Redis verification and Jenkins integration for operations-agent deploy pipeline blocked until cabinet returns.
Web Component decomposition scope not estimated. Could range from S (extract existing tabs as-is) to L (full semantic discovery with JSON-LD manifests).
Messaging layer decision deferred. Current polling-based architecture continues to function. No degradation from deferral.
Operations-agent cognitive architecture adaptation from platform/shared/ template untested. First agent to use the template — expect iteration.
